title: hirschfeldia incana subsp. adpressa
intro: Hirschfeldia incana subsp. adpressa, also known as Hoary Cress or Yellow Hirschfeldia, is a biennial plant in the mustard family that is native to Europe and Asia. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and pneumonia, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory compounds. The health benefits of Hirschfeldia incana subsp. adpressa include its ability to alleviate coughs, relieve congestion, and soothe sore throats, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies. Herbal preparations often involve the plant's leaves and flowers being used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
